Patch Set 1:

It looks like this runs sanity-sec test_31 correctly, but Maloo doesn't collect the test_31 logs because it is likely only looking for sanity-lnet files.  That isn't fatal, and I'd rather have the test running than spend a lot of time massaging it.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Looks good. The only problem I can see is that when sanity-sec test_31 is run from sanity-lnet, Maloo is not able to collect the associated logs. This can be seen here for instance:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/aa877833-ae80-4817-a904-5b8ac5a348a8

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> Looks good. The only problem I can see is that when sanity-sec test_31 is run from sanity-lnet, Maloo is not able to collect the associated logs. This can be seen here for instance:
> https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/aa877833-ae80-4817-a904-5b8ac5a348a8

I mentioned that in my earlier comment, and don't think it is critical to fix. I would rather have more testing than delay the patch to make the logging better (which would only be useful once the test is actually failing.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

parents.subject: LU-16216 tests: Update sanity-lnet for other LNDs

commit.subject: LU-16307 tests: run sanity-sec test_31 in sanity-lnet
commit.message:
LU-16307 tests: run sanity-sec test_31 in sanity-lnet

Since sanity-sec.sh test_31 is doing a lot of lnet-specific testing,
always run it as part of sanity-lnet.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-ldiskfs-ubuntu
Test-Parameters: testgroup=full-part-2
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I21160dbcfbf5fdd6be41b598b83c6bfacecfefb8
